key_points: Commons dorms are premium and fill fast; freshmen get first pick
The Hill is tier 2 and covered by VA benefits for eligible students
Off-campus is a common move after freshman year to escape rules
key_quotes: Too bad the freshman get first picks and commons get taken real fast!
With scholarships and VA benefits I will basically get free tuition and housing if it's tier 2 or less
My son does plan to move off but despite that he chose it and loves it

key_points: 'Ring by spring' marriage pressure is real and well-known on campus
Students forced by parents to attend skew the social dynamic heavily
Strict rules mean nightlife is essentially nonexistent in Lynchburg
key_quotes: 'ring by spring' is a slogan on campus
bro chose getting married over getting a degree... marriage idealist ahh liberty students
Entire town shuts down by 8. The only 'nightlife' is like overpriced restaurants

key_points: Shakesmart shake bar had menu and value slashed post-spring break
Plus swipes now only cover 4 flavors instead of previous variety
Students feel food system changes are going backward despite promised improvements
key_quotes: Shakesmart has been nerfed to the ground
Pre-spring break was a safe haven for people who wanted a good value for their plus swipes
Shake of the month costs $12 now???

key_points: AI plagiarism accusations are causing severe anxiety across the student body
Students report upping medication and feeling constantly on edge from false flags
The burden of proof falls entirely on students, with little institutional support
key_quotes: The AI has made my wife's life a living hell. She's an extremely talented writer and does it for a living
I have 2 bachelors and finishing this masters. Never in my life have I dealt with this! I am extremely overwhelmed
I do not think enough people are talking about the mental health toll that AI issues are placing on college students

key_points: Financial Aid department described as deeply inefficient with chronic delays
VA and military benefits are a genuine bright spot with good tuition discounts
Refunds are not automatically issued — you must explicitly request them
key_quotes: Someone needs to fix LU's Financial Aid Dept ASAP... it's the most inefficient nightmare. When DOE is faster, that says it all
Academic Advising helped me drop a course and told me I'd receive a full refund... 10 days later and nothing
They will also match up to about two thousand dollars of the Virginia Tuition Assistance Grant

key_points: Grading is wildly inconsistent — standards vary dramatically by professor
Biblical integration is embedded in every program, including secular degrees
AI detection enforcement is so aggressive it's undermining legitimate academic work
key_quotes: One professor will give 100s on all discussion boards. Then another professor will take off five points because you put bibliography instead of references
I know someone who took paralegal classes at Liberty. Like 70% of the questions were about the 'biblical basis' for the Constitution
I was blamed for using AI twice. One of the assignments I was accused of using AI for was an assignment about MYSELF AND MY LIKES AND DISLIKES

key_points: Turnitin AI detection is being weaponized with no reliable appeals process
Administration denies students exist to outside parties when caught rule-breaking
Canvas data hack was poorly communicated with no official email to students
key_quotes: If you are caught shoplifting LU will deny you are even a student. Even when the manager is holding the shoplifter's LU student ID
Why is there no official email or announcement on mylu or anything?
The professor was arrogant and clearly hostile toward me in her comments. She threatened to get higher ups involved if I disagreed

key_points: Campus itself is widely praised as beautiful and well-maintained
Liberty has bought and revitalized large portions of Lynchburg including the mall
Lynchburg is a small city with very limited entertainment options for young adults
key_quotes: The mall was honestly a run down ghost town a couple years ago, but now it is a place where people actually go
Entire town shuts down by 8
It's a gorgeous campus and the town was built around the college so there is a TON to do

key_points: Degree carries negative reputation in secular job markets, confirmed by multiple alumni
Graduates report having the Liberty degree joked about in actual job interviews
Employers in many fields view a Liberty degree as a liability, not a neutral credential
key_quotes: I avoid the 'where did you go to undergrad' question at all costs. I have included LU on my resume and literally had it lightly joked about in a job interview
99% of the time I would recommend follow the money. But having a degree from Liberty may not be worth the paper it is printed on
I have worked with colleagues and mentees who went to Liberty. They have universally had a horrible experience

key_points: Heavily discounted tuition for military and first responders is a genuine deal
Full-ride students and VA beneficiaries get strong financial value
For non-military paying full price, the reputational cost may outweigh tuition savings
key_quotes: With the military discount the whole program would cost me about $8k
I'm a cop and they gave 25% off! I love it
I'd generally avoid Liberty. There are lots of options for schools with reasonable tuition that won't come with the baggage

key_points: On-campus community is genuinely warm and tight-knit for committed Christians
Students who share the values love it; those who don't feel trapped
The school functions very differently for residential vs online students
key_quotes: Just graduated from Liberty last Spring... I had a wealth of friends caring for me in that time. People were staying with me until the latest hours of the night
This school was genuinely the downfall of my mental health from September to this last month
Only if you're very aligned with their values and mission

key_points: AI Detection: false positives are epidemic, affecting doctoral and grad students most
Reputation: secular employers actively penalize Liberty degrees in hiring
Sexual Misconduct: alleged rape victim was punished rather than supported by administration
Finance: financial aid office withholds refunds unless explicitly requested
key_quotes: one time a girl got raped and they blamed the girl and got her in trouble
Religion aside, Liberty is a joke in the academic community. If you're interested in grad school, I'd say to avoid it at all costs
I wonder if they realize they can be sued for defamation of character; additionally, they are going to start losing students

key_points: Convocation brings world leaders, athletes, and public figures to campus regularly
Military credit evaluation is generous — specific MOS training can substitute real courses
MA in Linguistics is one of the only fully online options of its kind in the US
key_quotes: Convocation is a phenomenal experience, including giving the entire student body the opportunity to see, hear, and even meet world leaders, sports stars, actors
They actually approved it to substitute for a specific engineering class... they evaluate military training pretty seriously
MA linguistics (theoretical, not applied) is exceptionally rare to find online. I'd say Liberty is the only university in the USA that offers it fully via distance
